title: Alan Greenspan, former Federal Reserve Chairman, dies at 100
summary: Considered a 'master' of monetary policy, his tenure was key to the US economy for 18 years.

title: Mutilated body found in freezer in Kobe; victim believed to have died 15 years ago
summary: Police identified the deceased as a former resident of the building and opened a homicide investigation.

title: Woman sentenced to 27 years in prison for murder of student in Asahikawa
summary: The court considered that the defendant acted with extreme cruelty, while the victim's family called the sentence insufficient.

title: US and Iran make progress in nuclear negotiations with international mediation
summary: The talks made progress on nuclear oversight and security in the Persian Gulf, although Iran took a demanding stance.

title: Reforms to Imperial Household Law advance to ensure succession
summary: The government submitted a draft that allows the adoption of males from the male line and ensures the continued presence of women in the imperial family.

title: Japan and US finance ministers hold discussions on exchange rates
summary: Minister Katayama and Treasury Secretary Bessent discussed the yen's movement in a virtual meeting amid bearish pressure on the Japanese currency.

title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
summary: Starmer submitted his resignation after a decline in popularity and internal pressure, opening a period of political uncertainty in the UK.

title: Prime Minister Takichi calls for removal of referendum clause in 'sub-capital' project
summary: Takichi proposed to the opposition to withdraw the expansion of voting to the entire prefecture for the plan to turn Osaka into a second capital.

title: Japan commemorates 81st anniversary of the end of the Battle of Okinawa
summary: At the Okinawa Memorial Day ceremony, Governor Denny Tamaki urged building a peaceful society.

title: Japan's emperor and empress begin state visit to Belgium
summary: The imperial couple is staying at Ciergnon Castle and will take part in official events with the Belgian royal family.

title: Yen falls to two-year low, surpassing 161 per dollar
summary: The Japanese currency weakened to 161.80 yen per dollar amid a global strengthening of the greenback.

title: Egypt achieves first World Cup victory in 92 years
summary: The Egyptian team defeated New Zealand with a goal from Mohamed Salah, sparking euphoria in Cairo.

title: JR East to operate first overnight Tokaido Shinkansen train in August
summary: The special service will depart from Tokyo to Shin-Osaka to offer an economical and efficient option for travelers.

title: Education Ministry orders inspection of firefighting equipment in schools after Tokyo fire
summary: A fire at a primary school in Kita-ku revealed possible failures in evacuation devices, leading to a national review.

title: Metropolitan Police orders detectives not to share information with potential stalkers
summary: For the first time in Japan, an order was issued for investigative agencies not to provide data that could facilitate stalking.

title: Six teenagers indicted for killing dog in robbery-homicide case in Tochigi
summary: The suspects allegedly used the same knife to kill the resident and the animal, according to the indictment.

title: Princess Aiko attends Japan Philharmonic concert for its 70th anniversary
summary: The emperor's daughter enjoyed Mahler's Symphony of a Thousand at the commemorative concert.

title: Aung San Suu Kyi's dog dies without leader being able to say goodbye
summary: The dog, which accompanied Suu Kyi before her detention, died while she remains under house arrest.

title: Fair Trade Commission sanctions Kawai for paying excessively low wages to instructors
summary: It was determined that the company violated the law by setting meager compensation for trial lessons at its music academies.

title: Miyū Yamashita wins first LPGA tournament of the season
summary: The Japanese golfer won in a playoff and climbed to seventh in the world rankings.
